title.zh: 英水中人面鱼身的怪鱼
title.en: Human-Faced Fish of the Ying River

summary.zh: 青丘山英水中的人面鱼，通体是鱼，面孔似人，叫声像鸳鸯；吃它的肉可以不生疥疮。
summary.en: A fish of the Ying River on Mount Qingqiu, wholly fish-bodied but with a human face and a call like a mandarin duck; eating it was said to prevent scabies.

description.zh:
英水发源于青丘之山，向南流入即翼之泽，水中多赤鱬。《南山经》说它"其状如鱼而人面，其音如鸳鸯，食之不疥"。它是一条长着人脸的鱼，名字里的"赤"暗示体色发红，叫声却温软如鸳鸯；功效很实在——吃了不生疥疮。它与九尾狐、灌灌同住青丘一山，一山之中兽、鸟、鱼各占一席。

人面鱼在《山海经》里自成谱系：《海内北经》的陵鱼"人面，手足，鱼身"，《海内南经》的氐人国之民人面鱼身而无足，《北山经》龙侯山的人鱼"如䱱鱼，四足"。赤鱬是这一谱系在南方水系的代表。讨论这类形象时，人们也常把西安半坡出土的人面鱼纹彩陶盆放在一旁对照——那是远早于此书成书的图像，却与经文里的人面鱼遥相呼应。后世中国式"人鱼"故事的种种源头里，总有赤鱬的一席。
description.en:
The Ying River rises on Mount Qingqiu and runs south into the Marsh of Jiyi, and in its waters swarm the chiru. The Classic of the Southern Mountains describes a creature shaped entirely like a fish but wearing a human face, its call like the soft cry of a mandarin duck; the 'chi' of its name suggests a reddish body. The prescribed benefit is bluntly practical: eat it and you will not suffer scabies. It shares Mount Qingqiu with the nine-tailed fox and the guanguan bird, the mountain fielding one marvel each for beast, bird, and fish.

Human-faced fish form a family of their own in the Shanhaijing: the lingyu of the northern seaboard has a man's face with hands and feet on a fish's body, the people of the Land of Diren are fish-bodied and legless, and the northern mountains hold four-legged 'man-fish'. The chiru is the southern representative of the line. Discussions of the motif often set beside it the human-face-and-fish painted basin unearthed at Banpo near Xi'an — an image millennia older than the book, yet uncannily close to its text — and among the sources of later Chinese mermaid lore the chiru keeps a firm place.

value.en: The Ying River and Marsh of Jiyi, Mount Qingqiu

sources.en: Shanhaijing, 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: North'

image.artist: Jiang Yinghao 蔣應鎬 (illustr.); Li Wenxiao 李文孝 (engr.)
